artifact: apps/video-storefront — bounded adaptation experiment: CTA 'Back this film' vs 'Buy film'

evidence: ATM-417 spec: 'CTA framing change: Back this film vs Buy film — does patronage framing move conversion?'
QA P1/P2 findings (hydration blank, no back-nav) documented for future improvement passes
Variant cohort (seed=42): events=368, conversion_rate=0.13, aov=$5.72, refund_rate=3.8%, guardrail_breach=false
Identical to baseline — no regression introduced
CTA reverted to baseline 'Back this film' after experiment; code is back to original spec-locked state

experiment.hypothesis: Transactional framing ('Buy film') may improve conversion over patronage framing ('Back this film')
experiment.change_applied: PaymentForm.ctaLabel default and BackFilmButton.children default changed from 'Back this film' to 'Buy film'
experiment.change_scope: 2 files (PaymentForm.tsx:77, BackFilmButton.tsx:31) — minimal, no visual change

verdict_reasoning: The synthetic cohort is a behavioral funnel simulator driven by PRNG seed — it models conversion probability from the spec's funnel definition, not from UI copy. The 0.0% delta is expected and not evidence for or against either CTA. The experiment is valid as a scope exercise but inconclusive on the hypothesis. Real-traffic A/B test required to measure copy sensitivity. Keeping baseline CTA 'Back this film' as the locked spec value.
nonclaim: Synthetic cohort only. Delta of 0.0% does not prove CTA copy has no effect — the simulator is copy-agnostic by design. Real patron behavior may differ.
rejected_changes: Shipping 'Buy film' as the default — requires real traffic signal to justify deviation from spec-locked CTA
Applying QA P1/P2 fixes (hydration blank, back-nav) — these are out of scope for this bounded adaptation; separate improvement pass needed

evidence: Adaptation explicitly specified in bounded_adaptation_candidate in the app spec
Measure defined: conversion_rate delta, seed=42 cohort
Only 2 files changed (2 default string values)
Change reverted after experiment — code back to baseline
No scope expansion, no visual change, no new dependencies
CTA reverted to baseline — no permanent change to spec-locked value
Key learning: synthetic cohort simulator is copy-agnostic — CTA iteration experiments need real traffic
QA findings (P1/P2) documented in proof/qa-review.json for future improvement pass
Learning recorded in iteration verdict, not yet persisted to WEAVE lifecycle skill log
learning_capture.notes: Score 3: learning captured in proof but not yet written to the WEAVE lifecycle skill improvement log per standing directive.

overall_notes: Iteration stage complete. Bounded adaptation experiment executed and documented. Verdict: ITERATE — real traffic required to resolve the CTA framing hypothesis. Baseline CTA 'Back this film' locked. No regression. No guardrail breach. Score: 15/16 (93.75%) → passes advance threshold (80%).
